comments.author: @Jim-Brown-g4w
comments.text: I visited Saint Thomas about 20 years ago and loved it !

comments.author: @GlobeExperiences2026
comments.text: St. Thomas is the ultimate tropical dream! 🇻🇮 This 4K travel documentary offers such a realistic and vibrant look at living in St. Thomas and the US Virgin Islands. The idea of living in an American Caribbean paradise with no passport needed for US citizens is a massive draw for expats and retirees. From the crystal-clear waters of Magens Bay to everyday island life around Charlotte Amalie, this video balances the breathtaking beauty with genuine insights on island time and living costs. Incredible footage!

comments.author: @rfr7039
comments.text: Born and raised in STT, Left in 1985. come back home occasionally.

comments.author: @travelnearme
comments.text: St. Thomas is not just a tropical paradise, but a place where family, community, and genuine resilience shape daily life. It makes me wonder what it would feel like to watch the sunset there after the cruise ships have left. Has anyone here ever truly experienced St. Thomas beyond the popular tourist spots. I would love to visit someday and see that authentic side for myself.

replies.author: @Jigganva
replies.text: There is a window in August that’s quieter because all the cruises ships are in Alaska!!  I stayed for 7 days during this time and got to see the island for what it is, paradise!!  Only drawback is the restaurants closed early for a vacation spot!!  The beaches on St. John was some of the most beautiful in the world.

comments.author: @EpicAdventureWorld
comments.text: If you had one free week in the Caribbean, would you spend all of it in St. Thomas or island-hop? I'd love to hear everyone's answer.

comments.author: @mejoresviajes6688
comments.text: This video beautifully captures the vibrant culture, daily life, and breathtaking Caribbean scenery of St. Thomas. Would you choose to live in this tropical paradise or only visit for a vacation?

comments.author: @LifeNest-3
comments.text: This looks nice !

comments.author: @lastlivingplaces
comments.text: This offers such a refreshing perspective on Argentina. The vintage aesthetic and slow travel pacing highlight the real, everyday culture beyond the stadiums. It feels incredibly genuine, showing the true heart of the local people.

comments.author: @Earthcue
comments.text: the colorful hillside neighborhoods look nothing like what you see on travel brochures… this is the St. Thomas I actually want to experience

comments.author: @darrenerskine8050
comments.text: I lived in St.THOMAS FOR 3YS

comments.author: @比較データ
comments.text: Life is lived at a human scale where taking a moment for connection is prioritized." What a beautiful mindset. We could all learn something from the island pace.

comments.author: @AloneAlex-d2l
comments.text: Turquoise waters and luxury yachts tell only half the story. ⛵ The real challenge of island life always lies in the infrastructure and cost of survival once the peak season ends. How sustainable is the current model for locals trying to build a permanent life there?

comments.author: @EdgeTalesDocs
comments.text: This documentary was such a beautiful look beyond the typical tourist experience in St. Thomas. It's so refreshing to see the real heart, culture, and resilience of the local community. Definitely makes me want to visit and support the local businesses even more! 🏝✨

comments.author: @prinscharles4817
comments.text: beautiful n peaceful  island 🎉 watching from India ❤️🙏

comments.author: @AmazingDiscoveriesTopUS
comments.text: Millions visit St. Thomas on cruise ships every year... But only a few ever experience the real island. Are tourists missing the best part?

comments.author: @deloresthomas5624
comments.text: I lived and taught in the St.Thomas for over forty years I appreciate the fact that I was able to see familiar places. You are right about people looking out for each other and developing a spirit of survival after hurricanes. I even saw some familiar faces and I thank you. Brought back great memories.

comments.author: @GoBeyondTravelTV
comments.text: If you had to live here for five years instead of just taking a vacation, would you still choose St. Thomas? Why or why not?

comments.author: @spcltee66
comments.text: Could have done better with actual research and sounding so generic..

comments.author: @mrstanbmw
comments.text: Very rich very poor most islands are like this some poorer than othets

comments.author: @RobertoBren
comments.text:
I live here in St. Thomas. 
Loved the cinematography, showed a lot of Charlotte Amalie and what tourist see. But not quite an accurate depiction what daily life is like. That being said, I wouldn't trade it for anywhere else. ✌️😎🏝

replies.author: @veerobinson6845
replies.text: I have never been to St Thomas, but looking at this blog, it's just as nice as St. Lucia, which I have visited twice in the past 15 years. I've got to say St Lucia is one of my least favourite of all the caribbean Islands and I've visited 10. That said, for anyone who hasn't been to St Lucia, I would recommend they do.
